no_host_msg.regex: No host is available on the machine template assigned for this job

children.judgment_q: n/a — structured: the standard (non-PersonalWorkspace) template assigned to the job has an empty robotVersions array, so zero runtimes are connected.

Is the local host one of the machines registered to the assigned (Default-scope) template? No uip command maps the local hostname to template membership — decide from gathered hostname evidence (Investigation steps 2/6). If the host is NOT a member of the assigned template, take leaf_not_on_assigned_machine; if it IS a member but its Assistant/runtime is offline, take leaf_no_host_connected.

nodes.cause: Job stuck Pending — 'No host is available on the machine template assigned for this job'. The assigned standard template (Default scope) has zero connected runtimes (robotVersions empty): no signed-in Assistant / Robot Service in Service or Unattended mode pointed at this Orchestrator URL + tenant. Re-triggering will NOT help — a fresh dispatch finds the same empty template and lands back in Pending.

Job targets a machine template the current host is not a member of — the user is on a different machine, or no host has ever connected to the assigned template. Connect to the assigned machine and restore Assistant connectivity, or reassign the job to a template that includes the current machine.

NOT no-host: the assigned template currently HAS a connected runtime (robotVersions populated). The PendingReasons.Errors/ErrorCodes are a stale snapshot from the original dispatch attempt. Remediation is to stop and re-trigger, not to provision a host.

nodes.reason: Host presence on the assigned template is ambiguous (mixed/partial robotVersions, or which template the job targets is unclear). Inspect machines list --all-fields per template and the job's PendingReasons before concluding no-host vs stale-dispatch. Do not treat empty Job.MachineKey as evidence — it is empty for every Pending job.
Pending job is not a no-available-host case (no TemplateNoHostsAvailable code, or job not Pending). Route to another orchestrator job-pending playbook or hand back to triage.
